items.title: Victorian government ordered to pay landowner $27m over rezoning

children.content: A court orders the Victorian government to pay more than $27 million in compensation to a landowner whose property was partially rezoned for public acquisition, after initially making the man a formal offer of $0.

items.title: Public transport to be free for Victorians in April

children.content: The Victorian government says public transport will be free across the state in April in a bid to relieve cost-of-living pressure as fuel prices continue to climb.

image.altText: Melbourne Metro city loop train

items.title: Former AFL footballer asked how much he drank before he was allegedly raped

children.content: Daniel Hayes was 17 years old when he was allegedly raped by his coach while playing in an Australian Rules football talent league in Victoria.

image.altText: An Indigenous man in front of a wooden fence with mottled light over his face.

items.title: Coalition, One Nation idea would be 'absolute disaster' for Victoria

children.content: Former premier Steve Bracks says One Nation teaming up with the Coalition would be a disaster for Victoria, and has hit out at Jeff Kennett for even suggesting it.

avatar.alt: Richard Willingham in front of a white background, stares straight down the barrel.

items.title: Scott Boland takes two wickets in two balls to put Victoria on top in Sheffield Shield final, live scores

children.content: South Australia needs a big recovery on day four of the Sheffield Shield if they are to set Victoria a competitive total at Melbourne's Junction Oval.

image.altText: Scott Boland celebrates

items.title: Police search for teens after Melbourne crash as paramedic injured

children.content: Police say four teenagers in a stolen car collided with an older couple in Carlton this morning, with two fleeing the scene immediately.

image.altText: Blue and white police tape at a crime scene.

items.title: The next step after treaty is here — and there's a lot at stake

children.content: Victoria's treaty elections are underway, with successful candidates taking on new responsibilities. But uncertainty remains, with the opposition sticking to its pledge to scrap the agreement if elected in November.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a singlet smiles as he reads a pamphlet.
An image of a woman looking at the camera, wearing a 'Treaty for Victoria' shirt.
A diagram showing Gellung Warl, the truth-telling and accountability institutions under Treaty

items.title: 'It's about fairness': Australian Iranians hit back at 'racist' visa ban

children.content: Thousands of Iranian travellers have been barred from entering Australia under a government ban targeting people who might overstay their visas, prompting criticism by refugee advocates.

image.altText: A family outdoors smiling

items.title: Offshore wind power to save billions of dollars and kilometres of powerlines

children.content: Developing Victoria's wind power sector could save the state money and powerline infrastructure, while providing stability for Eastern Australia's power grid, a new report shows.

image.altText: Turbines in an offshore wind farm in the ocean.

items.title: Porepunkah's darkest chapter explored in comedy festival show

children.content: David Quirk is adamant his new stand-up show, Porepunkah, won't make fun of last year's police shooting, but it wouldn't exist without it.

image.altText: A man on a stage

items.title: Complaints about foul smells skyrocket in Melbourne's west

children.content: Adam Pattison spent two years doing up his backyard to enjoy outdoor barbecues, but says "sickening" smells kill the vibe five times a week.

image.altText: Adam Pattison looking annoyed in his backyard

items.title: AFL players to lose insurance coverage for brain injuries

children.content: More than 500 AFL players will soon no longer be covered by insurance for brain injuries in their superannuation after Zurich Insurance moved to cut their ability to claim for concussion and head trauma.

articles.title: Magpies confirm 'hamstring awareness' for Moore after win over Giants

children.content: Collingwood is expecting to be without its skipper Darcy Moore and his predecessor Scott Pendlebury next week against Brisbane after the pair were hobbled during the win over GWS.

image.altText: Darcy Moore heads into the locker rooms with an injury

articles.title: AFL admits crucial umpire error late in Crows' defeat to Cats

children.content: Geelong's Tom Atkins convinced umpires a free-kick was his in a decision the AFL has since clarified should have gone Adelaide's way in a tight contest.

image.altText: Tom Atkins and Zac Taylor contest a ball on the ground near their feet

gallery.altText: A football on the ground during Hawthorn Hawks' training session at Waverley Park in 2008.

thumbnail.altText: A football on the ground during Hawthorn Hawks' training session at Waverley Park in 2008.

items.title: Adrian Portelli's company found guilty of unlawful lotteries by SA court

children.content: Melbourne investor Adrian Portelli has been found not guilty of illegal lottery charges but his company has been ordered to pay tens of thousands of dollars in fines.

image.altText: A man holding 100 dollar bills in his hand

items.title: 'Darkest day': Police mark 40th anniversary of Melbourne's Russell Street bombing

children.content: The 1986 car bomb attack on Victoria Police headquarters in Melbourne claimed the life of 21-year-old constable Angela Taylor and injured more than 20 others.

image.altText: The fiery wreckage of a car burns outside the police headquarters in Russell Street, in a still taken from file video footage.

items.title: Nazeem Hussain developed his 'gut-punch' comedy over a lifetime of racism

children.content: For comedian Nazeem Hussain, the best way to respond to war, discrimination and racism is to laugh about it. And he's built his whole career on this special brand of risky, outrageous comedy.

gallery.altText: Actor Nazeem Hussain stands smiling on a beach at sunset

thumbnail.altText: Actor Nazeem Hussain stands smiling on a beach at sunset

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: Victoria's top order wobbles as South Australia misses big chance in Shield final

children.content: Victoria and South Australia meet in the final of the Sheffield Shield at Melbourne's Junction Oval.

image.altText: Campbell Kellaway walks off the field as South Australian players celebrate the wicket

items.title: 16yo girl latest to be charged over Aidan Becker's death at Mernda station

children.content: A 16-year-old girl is the fifth person to be charged following the death of a 22-year-old man at a train station in Melbourne's outer north earlier this month.

image.altText: A photo of Aidan Becker smiling

items.title: Fire-ravaged shire demands fossil fuel companies foot climate disaster bill

children.content: A Victorian council still cleaning up after a bushfire that wiped out more than 50 homes and businesses wants fossil fuel companies to pay for climate disaster recovery.

gallery.altText: a white car has been completely burnt out by a fire
A man in a hat and hi-vis shirt amid burnt apple trees.

thumbnail.altText: a white car has been completely burnt out by a fire
A man in a hat and hi-vis shirt amid burnt apple trees.

items.title: Koala suffers fatal injuries after allegedly being dragged by car

children.content: Wildlife carers say a koala had to be euthanised after it was injured when it was allegedly dragged behind a car in Victoria's south west.

image.altText: Daisy the koala with friends at the Mosswood Wildlife Sanctuary in Koroit

items.title: Bay the 'greatest risk and biggest asset' for beachside residents in Victoria

children.content: For residents of Victoria's Bellarine Peninsula like Vicki, who is facing the impacts of climate change-induced erosion and inundation, the water near their doors is both their biggest asset and greatest risk.

image.altText: Vicki Perrett stands in her driveway with the ocean in the background.

items.title: Controversial Melbourne nightclub Love Machine destroyed by fire

children.content: Victoria Police are investigating a suspicious fire that tore through a controversial Melbourne nightclub.

image.altText: The exterior of a nightclub that was destroyed by fire

items.title: Here's who you should see at comedy festivals across Australia

children.content: Comedy season is in full swing, with festivals across the country hosting the best acts from Australia and the world. We asked our favourites to share their recommendations.

image.altText: A TV still of Aaron Chen, 30, Chinese Australian, in glasses, performing on stage, smiling and clasping a mic in both hands.

items.title: History-making jockey Michelle Payne honoured with statue at Flemington

children.content: Australian horse racing trailblazer Michelle Payne, the first woman to win the Melbourne Cup, has been honoured with a statue unveiled today.

gallery.altText: Former Melbourne Cup winner Michelle Payne poses next to a statue dedicated to her

thumbnail.altText: Former Melbourne Cup winner Michelle Payne poses next to a statue dedicated to her

items.title: How do you make friends in the country if you don't play sport?

children.content: From crafting to surfing, people of all ages are experimenting with new hobbies to meet pals in regional Victoria.

image.altText: Social connections 2025-11-03 11:11:00

items.title: NFL confirms historic MCG game to be played on AFL semifinal weekend

children.content: The first-ever regular-season NFL game played in Australia has had its date confirmed, with the Melbourne Cricket Ground to host the historic match in the middle of the AFL finals series.

image.altText: Los Angeles Rams tight end Colby Parkinson (84) is tackled by San Francisco 49ers safety Malik Mustapha (6)

items.title: Seasons clash as polar blast joins summer cyclone on Australia's weather map

children.content: A clash of seasons is developing on Australia's weather map this week as a tropical cyclone tracks towards the WA west coast and a polar blast with thunderstorms forms over the south-east.

image.altText: A graphic of Australia with a cyclone icon on WA's west coast and a blue mass above Tasmania and far south-east.

items.title: Man avoids jail after leaving child in cardboard box in car park

children.content: A 32-year-old man says he is "ashamed" of his actions after he left a child in a cardboard box in a regional Victorian car park while stealing the vehicle the toddler was in.

image.altText: a gravel car park

items.title: AFL concussion class action expands to more than 100 former players

children.content: Eight more footballers have joined a multimillion-dollar concussion class action against the AFL, tipping the total to over 100 former players who all claim they have been left brain-damaged by the game.

articles.title: Last-touch non-call labelled 'farce' as Geelong edges Adelaide in thriller

children.content: Geelong continues its streak over Adelaide at Kardinia Park, as one of the AFL's off-season rule changes is put squarely in the spotlight for the wrong reasons.

image.altText: Jack Martin is hugged by a teammate as he celebrates a goal

articles.title: Ryswyk says reaction to coming out has been 'overwhelming'

children.content: Former Brisbane Lions player Leigh Ryswyk says the support he received after becoming the first AFL player to come out as gay has "blown me away".

articles.title: AFL says new illicit drug policy is 'most stringent in Australian sport'

children.content: The AFL says its new illicit drugs policy, which includes biannual hair-testing of all players, is the most stringent in Australian sport.

image.altText: Darcy Moore and Andrew Dillon at a press conference

gallery.altText: Nick Stevens reaches for the football while playing for Carlton in an AFL match.

thumbnail.altText: Nick Stevens reaches for the football while playing for Carlton in an AFL match.

items.title: Suspicious fire at church due to be converted into a mosque

children.content: Police are investigating a fire at a 160-year-old heritage-listed church north of Melbourne that was being converted into a mosque by the local Muslim community.

image.altText: A fire damaged building.

items.title: Wine growers fear smoke taint will spoil harvest as planned burns begin

children.content: Just months after devastating bushfires, wine growers are concerned planned burns in regional Victoria pose a new risk to their grapes.

image.altText: A hand holding a bunch of grapes.

items.title: In Australia's most food insecure suburbs, lines for aid are doubling

children.content: Most areas across Australia are dealing with food insecurity as food relief services say demand is growing. It comes as experts warn the price of groceries are likely to increase due to the war in the Middle East.

image.altText: Ann Cooper, pictured centre, holding a box alongside other volunteers from food charity Cos We Care.

items.title: Man facing trial over Easey Street murders charged with rape

children.content: Prosecutors have reinstated a rape charge against accused Easey Street killer Perry Kouroumblis after a magistrate had earlier struck out the offence.

image.altText: A man in a light blue singlet sitting in front of plants.

items.title: How Melbourne ended up with the biggest comedy festival in the world

children.content: The very first Melbourne International Comedy Festival launched 40 years ago. And while it's kept its scrappy charm, things look pretty different today.

thumbnail.altText: Composite image of a very young Rove, Paul McDermott and Tom Gleeson all on stage

items.title: 'Birthkeeper' may be forced to give evidence about influencer's freebirth death

children.content: A coroner investigating the death of nutrition influencer Stacey Warnecke after a freebirth may compel her support worker to appear at the inquest.

image.altText: Stacey Warnecke, who died after giving birth at home, smiles for a publicity photo in her kitchen.

items.title: Regional charity struggles to rescue more than 100 cats from property

children.content: More than 100 cats, many in poor condition, have been found at a regional Victorian home.
